title: Natural gas stocks lower on expected inventory buildup; pending new building gas ban in NY
text: Natural gas stocks slipped in Tuesday's trading session as the June NYMEX contract was down another $0.074 to $2.244/MMBtu, following a $0.092 selloff on Monday, after forecasts continued to suggest the market should anticipate significant storage injections in the weeks ahead.   “Although offset by falling gas prices and increasing coal-to-gas switching, milder weather is opening the door to massive mid-May injections,” EBW Analytics Group analyst Eli Rubin said.
